1: //! REST API server for Automaton — Axum-based HTTP interface.
2: //! Exposes all CRUD operations via JSON API, backed by automaton-postgres (sqlx).

19: /// JWT auth middleware. If `AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET` env var is set, validates
20: /// Bearer tokens on all `/api/*` routes. If unset, allows all requests.
21: use axum::body::Body;
22: 
23: async fn auth_middleware(
24:     request: axum::http::Request<Body>,
25:     next: middleware::Next,
26: ) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {
27:     let jwt_secret = std::env::var("AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET").unwrap_or_default();
28:     if jwt_secret.is_empty() {
29:         return Ok(next.run(request).await);
30:     }
31: 
32:     let auth_header = request
33:         .headers()
34:         .get("Authorization")
35:         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
36:         .and_then(|v| v.strip_prefix("Bearer "))
37:         .map(|s| s.to_string());
38:     let token = auth_header.ok_or(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
39: 
40:     use jsonwebtoken::{decode, DecodingKey, Validation, Algorithm};
41:     let key = DecodingKey::from_secret(jwt_secret.as_bytes());
42:     let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);
43:     validation.validate_exp = true;
44:     decode::<serde_json::Value>(&token, &key, &validation)
45:         .map_err(|_| StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
46: 
47:     Ok(next.run(request).await)
48: }

50: /// Create a router with all API endpoints, backed by the given DB and build cache dir.
51: pub fn create_router(
52:     db: Arc<automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb>,
53:     data_dir: PathBuf,
54: ) -> Router {
55:     let build_cache_dir = data_dir.join("builds");
56:     let app_state = AppState { db, build_cache_dir, data_dir };
57: 
58:     Router::new()
59:         .route("/health", get(health_handler))
60:         .route("/api/scripts", get(list_scripts).post(create_script))
61:         .route("/api/scripts/:path", get(get_script))
62:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/build", post(build_script))
63:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/run", post(run_script))
64:         .route("/api/jobs", get(list_jobs).post(enqueue_job))
65:         .route("/api/runs", get(list_runs))
66:         .route("/api/variables", get(list_variables).post(set_variable))
67:         .route("/api/variables/:path", get(get_variable))
68:         .route("/api/resources", get(list_resources).post(set_resource))
69:         .route("/api/resources/:path", get(get_resource))
70:         .route("/api/webhooks/:trigger_id", post(webhook_handler))
71:         .route("/api/events/:trigger_id", post(event_handler))
72:         .route("/api/triggers", get(list_triggers).post(create_trigger))
73:         .route("/api/graph/nodes", post(add_node))
74:         .route("/api/graph/edges", post(add_edge))
75:         .route_layer(middleware::from_fn(auth_middleware))
76:         .with_state(Arc::new(app_state))
77: }

79: /// Start the API server on the given address.
80: pub async fn serve(
81:     addr: &str,
82:     database_url: &str,
83:     data_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
84: ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
85:     let db = automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb::connect(database_url).await?;
86:     let db = Arc::new(db);
87:     let data_dir = data_dir.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("./data"));
88:     let app = create_router(db, data_dir);
89: 
90:     let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(addr).await?;
91:     tracing::info!("Automaton API server starting on {addr}");
92:     axum::serve(listener, app).await?;
93:     Ok(())
94: }

123: async fn create_script(
124:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
125:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
126: ) -> Response {
127:     let path = body["path"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
128:     let source = body["source"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
129:     if path.is_empty() || source.is_empty() {
130:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "path and source are required");
131:     }
132:     let manifest = serde_json::json!({
133:         "name": path,
134:         "version": body.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0"),
135:         "summary": body.get("summary"),
136:         "timeout_ms": body.get("timeout_ms").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(30000),
137:     });
138:     let deps: Vec<DepRef> = body.get("depends_on")
139:         .and_then(|d| d.as_array())
140:         .map(|arr| arr.iter().filter_map(|v| v.as_str()).map(DepRef::new).collect())
141:         .unwrap_or_default();
142: 
143:     match state.db.register_script(&path, &source, "0.1.0", &manifest, &deps).await {
144:         Ok(hash) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"hash": hash, "path": path})),
145:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
146:     }
147: }
148:

150:     match state.db.list_scripts().await {

152:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
153:     }
154: }

167: async fn build_script(
168:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
169:     Path(path): Path<String>,
170: ) -> Response {
171:     // Look up the script source
172:     let script = match state.db.get_script(&path).await {
173:         Ok(Some(s)) => s,
174:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Script not found"),
175:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
176:     };
177: 
178:     let source = script.get("source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
179:     if source.is_empty() {
180:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Script has no source code");
181:     }
182: 
183:     // Build manifest from script metadata
184:     let manifest = automaton_core::AutomationManifest {
185:         name: path.clone(),
186:         version: script.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0").to_string(),
187:         entry: "main".to_string(),
188:         summary: None,
189:         description: None,
190:         timeout_ms: 30_000,
191:         retry: None,
192:         permissions: vec![],
193:         depends_on: vec![],
194:         resources: vec![],
195:         tags: vec![],
196:         require_approval: false,
197:         inputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
198:         outputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
199:     };
200: 
201:     // Build using cache
202:     let build_cache = automaton_build::BuildCache::new(&state.data_dir);
203:     match build_cache.build_rust(&path, source, &manifest) {
204:         Ok((hash, binary_path)) => {
205:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
206:                 "status": "built",
207:                 "hash": hash,
208:                 "binary": binary_path.to_string_lossy(),
209:             }))
210:         }
211:         Err(e) => {
212:             // Parse diagnostics for structured error feedback
213:             let diagnostics = automaton_build::BuildCache::diagnose(&e);
214:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
215:                 "status": "failed",
216:                 "error": e,
217:                 "diagnostics": diagnostics,
218:             }))
219:         }
220:     }
221: }

368: /// Receive an incoming event and enqueue a job for the matching event trigger.
369: /// Validates that the trigger is an Event type. The body is passed as args.
370: async fn event_handler(
371:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
372:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
373:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
374: ) -> Response {
375:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
376:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
377:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
378:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
379:     };
380: 
381:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
382:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
383:     }
384:     if trigger.get("trigger_type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) != Some("event") {
385:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Not an event trigger");
386:     }
387: 
388:     // Verify event_source match if configured on the trigger
389:     let expected_source = trigger.get("config")
390:         .and_then(|c| c.get("event_source"))
391:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
392:     if let Some(expected) = expected_source {
393:         let received = args.get("event_source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
394:         if received != expected {
395:             return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Event source mismatch");
396:         }
397:     }
398: 
399:     let target_path = trigger.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
400:     let target_is_flow = trigger.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
401:     let kind = if target_is_flow { "flow" } else { "script" };
402: 
403:     match state.db.enqueue(kind, target_path, &args).await {
404:         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
405:             "status": "event_queued",
406:             "job_id": job_id,
407:             "target": target_path,
408:         })),
409:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
410:     }
411: }
412: 
413: /// Receive an incoming webhook event and enqueue a job for the matching trigger.
414: /// The body is parsed as JSON and passed as args to the target automation.
415: /// If the trigger's config has `webhook_secret`, pass it as `webhook_secret` in the JSON body.
416: async fn webhook_handler(
417:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
418:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
419:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
420: ) -> Response {
421:     // Look up trigger by ID
422:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
423:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
424:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
425:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
426:     };
427: 
428:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
429:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
430:     }
431: 
432:     // Verify webhook secret if configured
433:     let expected_secret = trigger.get("config")
434:         .and_then(|c| c.get("webhook_secret"))

[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/automaton/README.md]
# Automaton — AI-Native Rust Automation Substrate

**Automaton** is a CLI-based, graph-native automation framework for AI agents to create, compose, and execute modular Rust workflows. It exposes its entire substrate through an MCP server built on the official [rmcp](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/rust-sdk) crate.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AI Agent (LLM)                        │
│  Uses MCP tools to create modules → plan → execute       │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │ MCP (stdio/SSE)
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-mcp (MCP Server)                │
│  module.* | workflow.* | graph.* | registry.* | run.*   │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-engine (Orchestrator)           │
│  Planner → Materializer → Executor                      │
│  Converts design graph → run DAG → execution            │
└──────┬──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
       │                          │
┌──────▼──────────┐    ┌──────────▼───────────────────┐
│ automaton-graph │    │ automaton-runtime             │
│ Property graph   │    │ Process sandbox, retry, I/O │
│ (SQLite-backed)  │    │                              │
└─────────────────┘    └──────────────────────────────┘
       │                          │
       └──────────┬───────────────┘
                  │
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-registry                    │
│  SQLite-backed module catalog + content-addressed    │
│  build cache + run history                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize workspace
automaton init

# Scaffold a module
automaton new github.issue_triage

# Build it
automaton build github.issue_triage

# Run it
automaton run github.issue_triage --input '{"repo": "user/repo"}'

# Plan a workflow from a module
automaton plan github.issue_triage --max-depth 5

# List modules
automaton list

# Show module details
automaton show github.issue_triage

# Inspect the graph
automaton graph nodes
automaton graph path module_a module_b

# Start MCP server (for AI agents)
automaton mcp

# Diagnostics
automaton doctor
```

## Workspace Layout

```
~/.local/share/automaton/     # Data directory
├── registry.db               # Module catalog (SQLite)
├── graph.db                  # Property graph store (SQLite)
├── builds/                   # Compiled binary cache
├── modules/                  # Uncompiled module sources
├── work/                     # Runtime working directory
└── tmp/                      # Temp execution artifacts
```

## Project Structure

```
crates/
├── automaton-core/           # Shared types: manifests, graph nodes, errors
├── automaton-sdk/            # #[automaton] proc macro + prelude
├── automaton-sdk-derive/     # Proc macro implementation
├── automaton-cli/            # CLI binary
├── automaton-engine/         # Planner, DAG materializer, executor
├── automaton-registry/       # SQLite-backed module + build + run DB
├── automaton-graph/          # SQLite-backed property graph store
├── automaton-mcp/            # MCP server (rmcp)
└── automaton-runtime/        # Child process runner, retry, timeout
```

## Graph Model

Two-layer architecture:

1. **Design Graph** (persistent property graph): Modules, Workflows, Triggers, Resources, Secrets, Capabilities — interconnected via labeled edges (`DEPENDS_ON`, `CALLS`, `TRIGGERS`, `USES_RESOURCE`, etc.)

2. **Run Graph** (materialized DAG for one execution): Compiled from design graph + context, verified acyclic via `petgraph::toposort`.

## MCP Surface (for AI agents)

| Tool                   | Description                            |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `module.create`        | Register a new automation module       |
| `module.build`         | Compile module to binary               |
| `module.validate`      | Validate manifest + source             |
| `module.run`           | Execute a module                       |
| `module.deprecate`     | Remove module                          |
| `workflow.plan`        | Discover dependencies, build run graph |
| `workflow.materialize` | Verify DAG validity (cycle check)      |
| `graph.query`          | Query nodes by kind                    |
| `graph.pathfind`       | Find paths between nodes               |
| `graph.add_edge`       | Wire module dependencies               |
| `registry.search`      | Search registered modules              |
| `resource.bind`        | Bind typed resources                   |
| `run.logs`             | Inspect run history                    |
| `run.retry`            | Retry failed execution                 |
| `system.health`        | Component health check                 |

## Module Authoring

```rust
use automaton_sdk::prelude::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Input {
    repo: String,
    issue_number: u32,
}

#[derive(Serialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Output {
    summary: String,
    priority: String,
}

#[automaton]
async fn main(ctx: Context, input: Input) -> anyhow::Result<Output> {
    // Your automation logic
    Ok(Output {
        summary: format!("Triaged issue #{} from {}", input.issue_number, input.repo),
        priority: "medium".to_string(),
    })
}
```

Manifest: `automation.yaml`

```yaml
name: github.issue_triage
version: 0.1.0
entry: main
summary: "Triage GitHub issues by priority"
timeout_ms: 30000
retry:
  max_attempts: 3
  delay_ms: 1000
  backoff: exponential
permissions:
  - github.read
resources:
  - github.api
depends_on:
  - llm.summarize
tags:
  - github
  - issue
  - triage
```

## Design Decisions

- **Rust-first**: Smallest binary size (~2.7 MB compiled) and runtime memory (~4 MB RSS)
- **SQLite-backed**: No Postgres dependency for local-first use
- **petgraph DAG**: Topological scheduling with cycle detection
- **Windmill-inspired packaging**: Code + YAML manifest, content-addressed builds
- **MCP-native control**: AI agents interact through the MCP protocol, not raw shell
- **Property graph**: Two-layer (design + run) enables recursive module composition
- **Incremental compilation**: Shared build cache, debug/release mode split

## License

MIT

53:     /// Run a module directly: compile (if needed) and execute
54:     pub async fn run_module(
55:         &self,
56:         name: &str,
57:         source: &str,
58:         manifest: &automaton_core::AutomationManifest,
59:         input: &serde_json::Value,
60:     ) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
61:         if let Some(ref cache) = self.build_cache {
62:             let (_hash, binary_path) = cache
63:                 .build_rust(name, source, manifest)
64:                 .map_err(|e| format!("Build failed: {e}"))?;
65:

67:             let retry = manifest.retry.as_ref();

72:                     .await

78:                     .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?

81:         } else {
82:             Err("No build cache configured".to_string())
83:         }

86:     /// Process a single job: dequeue, execute, complete.

89:         &self,

104:                 );

106:                 match registry.get(&target) {

169:     /// Start the worker pull loop.
170:     /// When concurrency > 1, spawns N independent polling tasks
171:     /// each opening its own Registry handle (SQLite WAL supports concurrent access).
172:     pub async fn start(
173:         &self,
174:         registry: &Registry,
175:         poll_interval_ms: u64,
176:     ) {
177:         let concurrency = self.concurrency.max(1);
178:         tracing::info!(worker = %self.name, concurrency = concurrency, "Worker starting");
179: 
180:         if concurrency == 1 {
181:             let poll_interval = Duration::from_millis(poll_interval_ms);
182:             loop {
183:                 if self.shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {
184:                     tracing::info!(worker = %self.name, "Worker shutting down");
185:                     break;
186:                 }
187:                 self.process_job(registry, poll_interval).await;
188:             }
189:         } else {
190:             self.run_concurrent(poll_interval_ms, concurrency).await;
191:         }
192:     }

194:     /// Concurrent mode: spawn N tasks, each with its own Registry handle.
195:     async fn run_concurrent(&self, poll_interval_ms: u64, concurrency: usize) {
196:         let shutdown = self.shutdown.clone();
197:         let name = self.name.clone();
198:         let data_dir = self.data_dir.clone();
199:         let build_cache = self.build_cache.clone();
200:         let runtime_config = RuntimeConfig::default();
201: 
202:         let mut set = JoinSet::new();
203: 
204:         for i in 0..concurrency {
205:             let shutdown = shutdown.clone();
206:             let worker_name = format!("{}-{}", name, i);
207:             let data_dir = data_dir.clone();
208:             let build_cache = build_cache.clone();
209:             let runtime = Runtime::new(runtime_config.clone());
210: 
211:             set.spawn(async move {
212:                 let registry = match Registry::open(&data_dir) {
213:                     Ok(r) => r,
214:                     Err(e) => {
215:                         tracing::error!(task = %worker_name, error = %e, "Failed to open registry");
216:                         return;
217:                     }
218:                 };
219: 
220:                 tracing::info!(task = %worker_name, "Worker task started");
221: 
222:                 loop {
223:                     if shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {
224:                         tracing::info!(task = %worker_name, "Shutting down");
225:                         break;
226:                     }
227: 
228:                     match registry.dequeue(&worker_name) {
229:                         Ok(Some(job)) => {
230:                             let job_id = job["id"].as_i64().unwrap_or(0);
231:                             let target = job["target_path"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
232:                             let args = job.get("args").cloned().unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
233: 
234:                             tracing::info!(task = %worker_name, job_id, target = %target, "Processing");
235: 
236:                             match registry.get(&target) {
237:                                 Ok(Some(module)) => {
238:                                     if module.built {
239:                                         let result = build_cache.as_ref().and_then(|cache| {
240:                                             cache.build_rust(&target, &module.source, &module.manifest).ok()
241:                                         });
242:                                         if let Some((_hash, binary_path)) = result {
243:                                             let timeout = module.manifest.timeout_ms;
244:                                             let retry = module.manifest.retry.as_ref();
245:                                             let run_result = if let Some(rc) = retry {
246:                                                 runtime.run_with_retry(&binary_path, &args, rc, timeout).await
247:                                             } else {
248:                                                 runtime.run_binary(&binary_path, &args, timeout).await
249:                                             };
250:                                             if run_result.is_ok() {
251:                                                 let _ = registry.record_run(
252:                                                     &uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
253:                                                     &target,
254:                                                     &args,
255:                                                 );
256:                                                 tracing::info!(job_id, "Completed");
257:                                             } else {
258:                                                 tracing::error!(job_id, "Failed");
259:                                             }
260:                                         }
261:                                     } else {
262:                                         tracing::warn!(target = %target, "Not built");
263:                                     }
264:                                 }
265:                                 Ok(None) => tracing::warn!(target = %target, "Not found"),
266:                                 Err(e) => tracing::error!(error = %e, "Registry error"),
267:                             }
268: 
269:                             if let Err(e) = registry.complete_job(job_id) {
270:                                 tracing::error!(job_id, error = %e, "Failed to complete job");
271:                             }
272:                         }
273:                         Ok(None) => {
274:                             tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_interval_ms)).await;
275:                         }
276:                         Err(e) => {
277:                             tracing::error!(task = %worker_name, error = %e, "Dequeue error");
278:                             tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_interval_ms)).await;
279:                         }
280:                     }
281:                 }
282:             });
283:         }
284: 
285:         while let Some(result) = set.join_next().await {
286:             if let Err(e) = result {
287:                 tracing::error!(error = %e, "Worker task failed");
288:             }
289:         }
290:     }

1: //! Flow DAG engine for Automaton.
2: //! Handles branch_one, branch_all, forloop, whileloop, failure_module execution.
3: //! When executed via the Runtime, modules are spawned as subprocesses with proper
4: //! state management and cross-step reference resolution.
5: 
6: use std::collections::HashMap;
7: use std::sync::Arc;
8: 
9: use automaton_core::backend::RegistryBackend;
10: use automaton_core::*;
11: use automaton_runtime::Runtime;
12: 
13: pub struct FlowEngine;
14: 
15: impl FlowEngine {
16:     /// Flatten a flow definition into an ordered list of executable steps
17:     /// with dependency resolution, handling branches and loops.
18:     pub fn flatten(flow: &FlowDefinition) -> Result<Vec<ExecStep>> {
19:         let mut steps = Vec::new();
20:         Self::flatten_steps(&flow.steps, &flow.default_retry, flow.default_timeout_ms, &mut steps, 0)?;
21:         Ok(steps)
22:     }
23: 
24:     fn flatten_steps(
25:         input: &[FlowStep],
26:         default_retry: &Option<RetryConfig>,
27:         default_timeout: u64,
28:         result: &mut Vec<ExecStep>,
29:         depth: usize,
30:     ) -> Result<()> {
31:         if depth > 10 {
32:             return Err(AutomatonError::Other("Flow nesting too deep (>10)".into()));
33:         }
34:         for step in input {
35:             let retry = step.retry.clone().or_else(|| default_retry.clone());
36:             match &step.kind {
37:                 FlowStepKind::Script => {
38:                     result.push(ExecStep {
39:                         id: step.id.clone(),
40:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Script,
41:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
42:                         input: step.input.clone(),
43:                         retry,
44:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
45:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
46:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
47:                         stop_if: step.stop_if.clone(),
48:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
49:                     });
50:                 }
51:                 FlowStepKind::Shell { command, shell } => {
52:                     result.push(ExecStep {
53:                         id: step.id.clone(),
54:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Shell { command: command.clone(), shell: shell.clone() },
55:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
56:                         input: step.input.clone(),
57:                         retry,
58:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
59:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
60:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
61:                         stop_if: step.stop_if.clone(),
62:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
63:                     });
64:                 }
65:                 FlowStepKind::Sleep => {
66:                     result.push(ExecStep {
67:                         id: step.id.clone(),
68:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Sleep,
69:                         script_path: None,
70:                         input: serde_json::json!({"duration_ms": step.sleep_after_ms.unwrap_or(1000)}),
71:                         retry: None,
72:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
73:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
74:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
75:                         stop_if: None,
76:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
77:                     });
78:                 }
79:                 FlowStepKind::BranchOne(branches) => {
80:                     let branch_steps: Vec<Vec<String>> = branches.iter().map(|b| {
81:                         let mut ids = Vec::new();
82:                         for bs in b {
83:                             ids.push(bs.id.clone());
84:                         }
85:                         ids
86:                     }).collect();
87:                     result.push(ExecStep {
88:                         id: format!("{}__branch_one", step.id),
89:                         kind: ExecStepKind::BranchOne(branch_steps),
90:                         script_path: None,
91:                         input: step.input.clone(),
92:                         retry: None,
93:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
94:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
95:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
96:                         stop_if: None,
97:                         failure_step: None,
98:                     });
99:                     for branch in branches {
100:                         Self::flatten_steps(branch, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
101:                     }
102:                 }
103:                 FlowStepKind::BranchAll(branches) => {
104:                     let branch_steps: Vec<Vec<String>> = branches.iter().map(|b| {
105:                         b.iter().map(|bs| bs.id.clone()).collect()
106:                     }).collect();
107:                     result.push(ExecStep {
108:                         id: format!("{}__branch_all", step.id),
109:                         kind: ExecStepKind::BranchAll(branch_steps),
110:                         script_path: None,
111:                         input: step.input.clone(),
112:                         retry: None,
113:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
114:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
115:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
116:                         stop_if: None,
117:                         failure_step: None,
118:                     });
119:                     for branch in branches {
120:                         Self::flatten_steps(branch, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
121:                     }
122:                 }
123:                 FlowStepKind::ForLoop { iterator, steps } => {
124:                     let body_ids: Vec<String> = steps.iter().map(|s| s.id.clone()).collect();
125:                     result.push(ExecStep {
126:                         id: format!("{}__forloop", step.id),
127:                         kind: ExecStepKind::ForLoop {
128:                             iterator: iterator.clone(),
129:                             body_ids: body_ids.clone(),
130:                             body_steps: steps.clone(),
131:                         },
132:                         script_path: None,
133:                         input: step.input.clone(),
134:                         retry: None,
135:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
136:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
137:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
138:                         stop_if: None,
139:                         failure_step: None,
140:                     });
141:                     // Flatten body steps for dependency resolution
142:                     Self::flatten_steps(steps, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
143:                 }
144:                 FlowStepKind::WhileLoop { condition, steps, max_iterations } => {
145:                     let body_ids: Vec<String> = steps.iter().map(|s| s.id.clone()).collect();
146:                     result.push(ExecStep {
147:                         id: format!("{}__whileloop", step.id),
148:                         kind: ExecStepKind::WhileLoop {
149:                             condition: condition.clone(),
150:                             max_iterations: *max_iterations,
151:                             body_ids: body_ids.clone(),
152:                             body_steps: steps.clone(),
153:                         },
154:                         script_path: None,
155:                         input: step.input.clone(),
156:                         retry: None,
157:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
158:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
159:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
160:                         stop_if: None,
161:                         failure_step: None,
162:                     });
163:                     Self::flatten_steps(steps, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
164:                 }
165:                 FlowStepKind::CallFlow { flow_path, input } => {
166:                     result.push(ExecStep {
167:                         id: step.id.clone(),
168:                         kind: ExecStepKind::CallFlow { flow_path: flow_path.clone(), input: input.clone() },
169:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
170:                         input: step.input.clone(),
171:                         retry: None,
172:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
173:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
174:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
175:                         stop_if: None,
176:                         failure_step: None,
177:                     });
178:                 }
179:                 FlowStepKind::FailureModule => {
180:                     result.push(ExecStep {

184:                         input: step.input.clone(),
185:                         retry: None,

187:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
188:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
189:                         stop_if: None,
190:                         failure_step: None,
191:                     });
192:                 }
193:             }
194:         }
195:         Ok(())
196:     }

198:     /// Execute a flow using the Runtime for actual module subprocess execution.
199:     /// Resolves `$var:/$res:` references via the Registry, evaluates loops,
200:     /// and manages cross-step flow state.
201:     /// This is the backward-compatible 4-param version (failure_handler = None).
202:     pub async fn execute(
203:         steps: &[ExecStep],
204:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
205:         runtime: &Runtime,
206:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
207:     ) -> Result<Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)>> {
208:         Self::execute_with_handlers(steps, backend, runtime, build_cache_dir, None).await
209:     }

211:     /// Like `execute()` but with an optional failure_handler module path.
212:     /// When a step fails and `failure_handler` is set (and the step has no
213:     /// per-step `failure_step`), a synthetic FailureModule step is recorded
214:     /// and execution continues instead of erroring out.
215:     ///
216:     /// This also executes all independent (dependency-met) steps concurrently
217:     /// via `futures::future::join_all`.
218:     pub async fn execute_with_handlers(
219:         steps: &[ExecStep],
220:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
221:         runtime: &Runtime,
222:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
223:         failure_handler: Option<&str>,
224:     ) -> Result<Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)>> {
225:         let mut results: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> = HashMap::new();
226:         let mut completed = std::collections::HashSet::<String>::new();
227:         let mut pending: std::collections::VecDeque<usize> = (0..steps.len()).collect();
228:         let mut max_rounds = 1000;
229:         // Track completion order for deterministic output
230:         let mut completion_order: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
231: 
232:         while !pending.is_empty() && max_rounds > 0 {
233:             max_rounds -= 1;
234: 
235:             // Separate ready steps from not-ready steps
236:             let mut ready = Vec::new();
237:             let mut not_ready = Vec::new();
238:             while let Some(idx) = pending.pop_front() {
239:                 if steps[idx].depends_on.iter().all(|d| completed.contains(d)) {
240:                     ready.push(idx);
241:                 } else {
242:                     not_ready.push(idx);
243:                 }
244:             }
245:             pending.extend(not_ready);
246: 
247:             if ready.is_empty() {
248:                 // All remaining steps have unmet deps — deadlock
249:                 break;
250:             }
251: 
252:             // Pre-resolve $var:/$res: references for all ready steps
253:             let mut resolved_inputs = Vec::with_capacity(ready.len());
254:             for &idx in &ready {
255:                 let step = &steps[idx];
256:                 let ri = if let Some(be) = &backend {
257:                     be.resolve_references(&step.input).await
258:                         .unwrap_or_else(|_| step.input.clone())
259:                 } else {
260:                     step.input.clone()
261:                 };
262:                 resolved_inputs.push(ri);
263:             }
264: 
265:             // Build futures for all ready steps, handling stop_if inline
266:             let mut futs = Vec::with_capacity(ready.len());
267:             let mut skip_results: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = Vec::new();
268: 
269:             for (&idx, ri) in ready.iter().zip(resolved_inputs.iter()) {
270:                 let step = &steps[idx];
271: 
272:                 // Check stop_if before dispatching
273:                 let should_skip = step.stop_if.as_ref()
274:                     .map(|cond| evaluate_condition(cond, &results))
275:                     .unwrap_or(false);
276:                 if should_skip {
277:                     let reason = step.stop_if.as_deref().unwrap_or("stop_if_triggered");
278:                     skip_results.push((
279:                         step.id.clone(),
280:                         serde_json::json!({"status": "skipped", "reason": reason}),
281:                     ));
282:                     continue;
283:                 }
284: 
285:                 // Resolve cross-step state references
286:                 let resolved_input = crate::resolve_state_refs(ri, &results);
287: 
288:                 // Clone data for the async future (avoid borrow conflicts)
289:                 let step_clone = step.clone();
290:                 let steps_vec = steps.to_vec();
291:                 let bcd = build_cache_dir.to_path_buf();
292:                 let be_clone = backend.cloned();
293:                 let results_snapshot = results.clone();
294:                 let step_id = step.id.clone();
295: 
296:                 futs.push(async move {
297:                     let outcome = Self::execute_step_inner(
298:                         &step_clone,
299:                         &resolved_input,
300:                         &results_snapshot,
301:                         runtime,
302:                         &bcd,
303:                         be_clone.as_ref(),
304:                         &steps_vec,
305:                     ).await;
306:                     (step_id, outcome)
307:                 });
308:             }
309: 
310:             // Process skipped results immediately
311:             for (id, val) in skip_results {
312:                 completed.insert(id.clone());
313:                 completion_order.push(id.clone());
314:                 results.insert(id, val);
315:             }
316: 
317:             // Execute all ready steps concurrently
318:             let outcomes: Vec<(String, std::result::Result<serde_json::Value, AutomatonError>)> =
319:                 futures::future::join_all(futs).await;
320: 
321:             for (step_id, outcome) in outcomes {
322:                 match outcome {
323:                     Ok(output) => {
324:                         completed.insert(step_id.clone());
325:                         completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
326:                         results.insert(step_id, output);
327:                     }
328:                     Err(e) => {
329:                         // Check per-step failure_step first
330:                         let step_info = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == step_id).ok_or_else(||
331:                             AutomatonError::Other(format!("Step '{step_id}' not found after execution"))
332:                         )?;
333:                         if let Some(fallback) = &step_info.failure_step {
334:                             completed.insert(step_id.clone());
335:                             completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
336:                             results.insert(step_id, serde_json::json!({
337:                                 "error": e.to_string(),
338:                                 "fallback": fallback,
339:                             }));
340:                         } else if let Some(handler) = failure_handler {
341:                             // Use the on_failure handler — record a synthetic FailureModule
342:                             let handler_id = "__on_failure__";
343:                             completed.insert(step_id.clone());
344:                             completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
345:                             results.insert(step_id.clone(), serde_json::json!({
346:                                 "error": e.to_string(),
347:                                 "handler_triggered": handler,
348:                             }));
349:                             completed.insert(handler_id.to_string());
350:                             completion_order.push(handler_id.to_string());
351:                             results.insert(handler_id.to_string(), serde_json::json!({
352:                                 "status": "failure_handler_ready",
353:                                 "handled_error": e.to_string(),
354:                                 "for_step": step_id,
355:                                 "handler": handler,
356:                             }));
357:                         } else {
358:                             return Err(e);
359:                         }
360:                     }
361:                 }
362:             }
363:         }
364: 
365:         // Return results in completion order (HashMap has undefined iteration order)
366:         let ordered: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = completion_order.iter()
367:             .filter_map(|id| results.remove(id).map(|v| (id.clone(), v)))
368:             .collect();
369:         Ok(ordered)
370:     }

372:     /// Execute a SINGLE step with the given resolved input and state snapshot.
373:     /// Returns the output value for this step.
374:     async fn execute_step_inner(
375:         step: &ExecStep,
376:         resolved_input: &serde_json::Value,
377:         results_snapshot: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
378:         runtime: &Runtime,
379:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
380:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
381:         steps: &[ExecStep],
382:     ) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
383:         match &step.kind {
384:             ExecStepKind::Script => {
385:                 let script_path = step.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
386:                 let binary_path = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
387:                 if binary_path.exists() {
388:                     if let Some(retry_cfg) = &step.retry {
389:                         runtime.run_with_retry(&binary_path, resolved_input, retry_cfg, step.timeout_ms).await
390:                     } else {
391:                         runtime.run_binary(&binary_path, resolved_input, step.timeout_ms).await
392:                     }
393:                 } else {
394:                     Ok(serde_json::json!({"status": "no_binary_found", "path": script_path}))
395:                 }
396:             }
397:             ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } => {
398:                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
399: 
400:                 // Shell execution helper with kill_on_drop for orphan cleanup
401:                 let run_shell = || async {
402:                     let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_millis(step.timeout_ms);
403:                     let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
404:                         .arg("-c")
405:                         .arg(command.as_str())
406:                         .kill_on_drop(true)
407:                         .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
408:                         .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
409:                         .spawn()
410:                         .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("Failed to spawn shell: {e}")))?;
411: 
412:                     match tokio::time::timeout(timeout, child.wait_with_output()).await {
413:                         Ok(Ok(output)) => {
414:                             if output.status.success() {
415:                                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
416:                                     "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
417:                                     "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
418:                                     "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
419:                                 }))
420:                             } else {
421:                                 let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
422:                                 let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
423:                                 Err(AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!(
424:                                     "Shell command failed (exit {}): {}",
425:                                     output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
426:                                     if stderr.is_empty() { &stdout } else { &stderr },
427:                                 )))
428:                             }
429:                         }
430:                         Ok(Err(e)) => Err(AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("Shell process error: {e}"))),
431:                         Err(_) => Err(AutomatonError::Timeout(step.timeout_ms)),
432:                     }
433:                 };
434: 
435:                 // Run with optional retry
436:                 if let Some(retry_cfg) = &step.retry {
437:                     let mut last_error = String::new();
438:                     let mut delay = retry_cfg.delay_ms;
439:                     let mut output = None;
440:                     for attempt in 1..=retry_cfg.max_attempts {
441:                         match run_shell().await {
442:                             Ok(out) => { output = Some(out); break; }
443:                             Err(e) => {
444:                                 last_error = e.to_string();
445:                                 if attempt < retry_cfg.max_attempts {
446:                                     if delay > 0 {
447:                                         tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay)).await;
448:                                     }
449:                                     delay = match retry_cfg.backoff {
450:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Fixed => retry_cfg.delay_ms,
451:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Linear => retry_cfg.delay_ms * (attempt as u64 + 1),
452:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Exponential => retry_cfg.delay_ms * (1u64 << attempt),
453:                                     };
454:                                 }
455:                             }
456:                         }
457:                     }
458:                     output.ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(
459:                         format!("All {} attempts failed. Last error: {last_error}", retry_cfg.max_attempts)
460:                     ))
461:                 } else {
462:                     run_shell().await
463:                 }
464:             }
465:             ExecStepKind::Sleep => {
466:                 let ms = step.sleep_after_ms.unwrap_or(1000);
467:                 tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(ms)).await;
468:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({"slept_ms": ms}))
469:             }
470:             ExecStepKind::BranchOne(branches) => {
471:                     let mut branch_result = None;
472:                 for branch_ids in branches {
473:                     for bid in branch_ids {
474:                         if let Some(bstep) = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *bid) {
475:                             let script_path = bstep.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
476:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
477:                             if bp.exists() {
478:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, resolved_input, bstep.timeout_ms).await {
479:                                     Ok(r) => { branch_result = Some(r); break; }
480:                                     Err(_) => continue,
481:                                 }
482:                             } else if let ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &bstep.kind {
483:                                 // Shell-based fallback: first branch that succeeds
484:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
485:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
486:                                     .arg("-c")
487:                                     .arg(command.as_str())
488:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
489:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
490:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
491:                                     .spawn()
492:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("BranchOne shell: {e}")))?;
493:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(bstep.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
494:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
495:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) if output.status.success() => {
496:                                         branch_result = Some(serde_json::json!({
497:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
498:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
499:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
500:                                         }));
501:                                         break;
502:                                     }
503:                                     _ => continue,
504:                                 }
505:                             }
506:                         }
507:                     }
508:                     if branch_result.is_some() { break; }
509:                 }
510:                 Ok(branch_result.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({"error": "all_branches_failed"})))
511:             }
512:             ExecStepKind::BranchAll(branches) => {
513:                 let mut all_results = Vec::new();
514:                 for branch_ids in branches {
515:                     for bid in branch_ids {
516:                         if let Some(bstep) = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *bid) {
517:                             let script_path = bstep.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
518:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
519:                             if bp.exists() {
520:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, resolved_input, bstep.timeout_ms).await {
521:                                     Ok(r) => all_results.push(r),
522:                                     Err(e) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
523:                                 }
524:                             } else if let ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &bstep.kind {
525:                                 // Shell-based fallback for all branches
526:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
527:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
528:                                     .arg("-c")
529:                                     .arg(command.as_str())
530:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
531:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
532:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
533:                                     .spawn()
534:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("BranchAll shell: {e}")))?;
535:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(bstep.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
536:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
537:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) => {
538:                                         all_results.push(serde_json::json!({
539:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
540:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
541:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
542:                                         }));
543:                                     }
544:                                     Ok(Err(e)) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
545:                                     Err(_) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": "timeout"})),
546:                                 }
547:                             }
548:                         }
549:                     }
550:                 }
551:                 Ok(serde_json::json!(all_results))
552:             }
553:             ExecStepKind::ForLoop { iterator, body_ids, body_steps } => {
554:                 let mut local_results = results_snapshot.clone();
555:                 let iterable = resolve_iterable(resolved_input, results_snapshot.get(iterator));
556:                 let mut loop_results = Vec::new();
557:                 for (i, item) in iterable.iter().enumerate() {
558:                     local_results.insert(format!("{}__item", iterator), item.clone());
559:                     local_results.insert(format!("{}__index", iterator), serde_json::json!(i));
560:                     for body_id in body_ids {
561:                         if let Some(body_step) = body_steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *body_id) {
562:                             let script_path = body_step.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
563:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
564:                             if bp.exists() {
565:                                 let body_input = crate::resolve_state_refs(&body_step.input, &local_results);
566:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, &body_input, body_step.timeout_ms).await {
567:                                     Ok(r) => { local_results.insert(body_id.clone(), r.clone()); loop_results.push(r); }
568:                                     Err(e) => { loop_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})); }
569:                                 }
570:                             } else if let FlowStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &body_step.kind {
571:                                 // Shell-based fallback for loop body
572:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
573:                                 let resolved_cmd = crate::resolve_state_refs(
574:                                     &serde_json::json!(command.as_str()), &local_results,
575:                                 );
576:                                 let cmd_str = resolved_cmd.as_str().unwrap_or(command);
577:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
578:                                     .arg("-c")
579:                                     .arg(cmd_str)
580:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
581:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
582:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
583:                                     .spawn()
584:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("ForLoop shell: {e}")))?;
585:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(body_step.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
586:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
587:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) if output.status.success() => {
588:                                         let val = serde_json::json!({
589:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
590:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
591:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
592:                                         });
593:                                         local_results.insert(body_id.clone(), val.clone());
594:                                         loop_results.push(val);
595:                                     }
596:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) => {
597:                                         let err = format!("exit {}", output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1));
598:                                         loop_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": err}));
599:                                     }
600:                                     Ok(Err(e)) => loop_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
601:                                     Err(_) => loop_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": "timeout"})),
602:                                 }
603:                             }
604:                         }
605:                     }
606:                 }
607:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
608:                     "iterations": iterable.len(),
609:                     "results": loop_results,
610:                 }))
611:             }
612:             ExecStepKind::WhileLoop { condition, max_iterations, body_ids, body_steps } => {
613:                 let mut local_results = results_snapshot.clone();
614:                 let mut while_results = Vec::new();
615:                 let mut iteration = 0usize;
616:                 while evaluate_condition(condition, &local_results) && iteration < *max_iterations as usize {
617:                     for body_id in body_ids {
618:                         if let Some(body_step) = body_steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *body_id) {
619:                             let script_path = body_step.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
620:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
621:                             if bp.exists() {
622:                                 let body_input = crate::resolve_state_refs(&body_step.input, &local_results);
623:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, &body_input, body_step.timeout_ms).await {
624:                                     Ok(r) => { local_results.insert(body_id.clone(), r.clone()); while_results.push(r); }
625:                                     Err(e) => { while_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})); }
626:                                 }
627:                             } else if let FlowStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &body_step.kind {
628:                                 // Shell-based fallback for while body
629:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
630:                                 let resolved_cmd = crate::resolve_state_refs(
631:                                     &serde_json::json!(command.as_str()), &local_results,
632:                                 );
633:                                 let cmd_str = resolved_cmd.as_str().unwrap_or(command);
634:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
635:                                     .arg("-c")
636:                                     .arg(cmd_str)
637:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
638:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
639:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
640:                                     .spawn()
641:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("WhileLoop shell: {e}")))?;
642:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(body_step.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
643:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
644:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) if output.status.success() => {
645:                                         let val = serde_json::json!({
646:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
647:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
648:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
649:                                         });
650:                                         local_results.insert(body_id.clone(), val.clone());
651:                                         while_results.push(val);
652:                                     }
653:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) => {
654:                                         let err = format!("exit {}", output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1));
655:                                         while_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": err}));
656:                                     }
657:                                     Ok(Err(e)) => while_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
658:                                     Err(_) => while_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": "timeout"})),
659:                                 }
660:                             }
661:                         }
662:                     }
663:                     iteration += 1;
664:                 }
665:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
666:                     "iterations": iteration,
667:                     "results": while_results,
668:                 }))
669:             }
670:             ExecStepKind::FailureModule => {
671:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({"status": "failure_handler_ready"}))
672:             }
673:             ExecStepKind::CallFlow { flow_path, .. } => {
674:                 let be = backend.ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::Other(
675:                     format!("CallFlow '{flow_path}': no backend available for flow lookup")
676:                 ))?;
677:                 let flow_val = be.get_flow(flow_path).await?
678:                     .ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::Other(
679:                         format!("CallFlow '{flow_path}': flow not found")
680:                     ))?;
681:                 let def: FlowDefinition = serde_json::from_value(
682:                     flow_val.get("definition")
683:                         .cloned()
684:                         .ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::Other(
685:                             format!("CallFlow '{flow_path}': missing 'definition' field")
686:                         ))?
687:                 )?;
688:                 let child_steps = Self::flatten(&def)?;
689:                 let child_results = Self::execute_with_handlers(
690:                     &child_steps,
691:                     backend,
692:                     runtime,
693:                     build_cache_dir,
694:                     None,
695:                 ).await?;
696:                 let merged: serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value> = child_results.into_iter().collect();
697:                 Ok(serde_json::Value::Object(merged))
698:             }
699:         }
700:     }
701: }

703: /// Resolve an iterator value from a ForLoop definition.
704: /// Checks the resolved_input for an array, then falls back to upstream flow state.
705: fn resolve_iterable(resolved_input: &serde_json::Value, upstream: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
706:     // First check the flow input
707:     if let Some(arr) = resolved_input.as_array() {
708:         return arr.clone();
709:     }
710:     // Then check upstream state
711:     if let Some(up) = upstream {
712:         if let Some(arr) = up.as_array() {
713:             return arr.clone();
714:         }
715:         // If it's an object with an 'items' or 'results' field
716:         if let Some(items) = up.get("items").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
717:             return items.clone();
718:         }
719:         if let Some(results) = up.get("results").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
720:             return results.clone();
721:         }
722:         // Wrap scalar in a single-element vec
723:         if !up.is_null() {
724:             return vec![up.clone()];
725:         }
726:     }
727:     // Default: single iteration with empty object
728:     vec![serde_json::json!({})]
729: }
730: 
731: /// Evaluate a WhileLoop condition expression against current flow state.
732: /// Supports simple expressions like `${step.status} == "completed"`.
733: fn evaluate_condition(condition: &str, results: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>) -> bool {
734:     // Simple evaluation: resolve state refs then check equality
735:     let cond = condition.trim();
736:     if cond.is_empty() {
737:         return false;
738:     }
739: 
740:     // If the condition is just a state reference like `${step.status}`,
741:     // check if it resolves to a truthy value
742:     if cond.starts_with("${") && cond.ends_with("}") && !cond.contains(' ') {
743:         let ref_val = crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(cond), results);
744:         return ref_val.is_object() || ref_val.is_array() || ref_val.as_bool() == Some(true)
745:             || ref_val.as_str().map(|s| s == "true" || s == "completed" || s == "ok").unwrap_or(false)
746:             || (ref_val.is_number() && ref_val.as_f64().map(|v| v > 0.0).unwrap_or(false));
747:     }
748: 
749:     // Simple string comparison: "${x.status} == completed"
750:     if let Some(eq_pos) = cond.find("==") {
751:         let left_str = cond[..eq_pos].trim();
752:         let right_str = cond[eq_pos + 2..].trim().trim_matches('"');
753:         let left_val = if left_str.starts_with("${") {
754:             crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(left_str), results)
755:         } else {
756:             serde_json::json!(left_str)
757:         };
758:         let left_display = left_val.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string())
759:             .or_else(|| left_val.as_f64().map(|n| n.to_string()))
760:             .unwrap_or_default();
761:         return left_display == right_str;
762:     }
763: 
764:     // Simple string inequality: "${x.count} > 0"
765:     if let Some(gt_pos) = cond.find('>') {
766:         let left_str = cond[..gt_pos].trim();
767:         let right_str = cond[gt_pos + 1..].trim();
768:         let left_val = if left_str.starts_with("${") {
769:             crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(left_str), results)
770:         } else {
771:             serde_json::json!(left_str)
772:         };
773:         let left_num = left_val.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
774:         let right_num: f64 = right_str.parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
775:         return left_num > right_num;
776:     }
777: 
778:     // Default: resolve the condition as a state ref and check truthiness
779:     let resolved = crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(cond), results);
780:     resolved.as_bool() == Some(true)
781:         || resolved.as_str().map(|s| s == "true" || s == "1").unwrap_or(false)
782:         || resolved.as_f64().map(|v| v > 0.0).unwrap_or(false)
783: }
784: 
785: /// A step ready for execution after flattening
786: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
787: pub struct ExecStep {
788:     pub id: String,
789:     pub kind: ExecStepKind,
790:     pub script_path: Option<String>,
791:     pub input: serde_json::Value,
792:     pub retry: Option<RetryConfig>,
793:     pub timeout_ms: u64,
794:     pub depends_on: Vec<String>,
795:     pub sleep_after_ms: Option<u64>,
796:     pub stop_if: Option<String>,
797:     pub failure_step: Option<String>,
798: }
799: 
800: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
801: pub enum ExecStepKind {
802:     Script,
803:     Shell {
804:         command: String,
805:         shell: Option<String>,
806:     },
807:     Sleep,
808:     BranchOne(Vec<Vec<String>>),
809:     BranchAll(Vec<Vec<String>>),
810:     ForLoop {
811:         iterator: String,
812:         body_ids: Vec<String>,
813:         body_steps: Vec<FlowStep>,
814:     },
815:     WhileLoop {
816:         condition: String,
817:         max_iterations: u32,
818:         body_ids: Vec<String>,
819:         body_steps: Vec<FlowStep>,
820:     },
821:     FailureModule,
822:     CallFlow {
823:         flow_path: String,
824:         input: Option<serde_json::Value>,
825:     },
826: }
827: 
828: #[cfg(test)]
829: mod tests {
830:     use super::*;
831: 
832:     #[test]
833:     fn test_evaluate_condition_truthy_state_ref() {
834:         let mut results = HashMap::new();
835:         results.insert("step_a".into(), serde_json::json!({"status": "completed"}));
836:         assert!(evaluate_condition("${step_a.status}", &results));
837:     }
838: 
839:     #[test]
840:     fn test_evaluate_condition_eq_comparison() {

843:         assert!(evaluate_condition(r#"${step_a.status} == "completed""#, &results));
844:         assert!(!evaluate_condition(r#"${step_a.status} == "failed""#, &results));
845:     }

851:         assert!(evaluate_condition("${step_a.count} > 0", &results));
852:         assert!(!evaluate_condition("${step_a.count} > 10", &results));
853:     }

1: # Automaton — AI-Handover Document
2: 
3: **Generated:** 2026-05-07 (Post Full-Scale Refactor)  
4: **GitHub:** https://github.com/ishan-parihar/automaton  
5: **Purpose:** A Rust-native automation substrate for AI agents to create, compose, and manage their own infrastructure through MCP tools.  
6: **Architecture n8n-for-AI-agents:** Modular Rust scripts composed into complex hierarchical flows with node-level interconnections, compiled and validated before execution.
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## 1. Architecture Overview
11: 
12: ```
13: crates/
14: ├── automaton-core/         — Shared types: manifests, graph nodes, flows, errors, secrets, triggers, jobs, encryption
15: ├── automaton-sdk/          — #[automaton] proc macro for agent-authored modules

17: ├── automaton-engine/       — DAG planner + FlowEngine (branch_one, branch_all, forloop, whileloop)
18: ├── automaton-runtime/      — Subprocess runner with retry + timeout (retry delay bug fixed)
19: ├── automaton-graph/        — SQLite-backed property graph store (using automaton-core[rusqlite])

21: ├── automaton-build/        — Real cargo build integration, SDK auto-detection, extra deps, template system

23: ├── automaton-scheduler/    — Cron expression validation + ticker + trigger persistence
24: ├── automaton-mcp/          — MCP server with 29 tools, real JSON schemas, all tools persist data
25: ├── automaton-postgres/     — Postgres backend via sqlx (PgPool, all CRUD operations)
